evals.prompt: Characterize the vibe of my group chat with substring <GROUP_NAME>. Give me the top themes, the dynamic between participants, and 3-5 representative verbatim quotes with timestamps and senders.
evals.expected_output: A concise characterization with verbatim-quoted evidence. The skill should run chats/stats/participants to ground the summary, then use anchor-sweep or window to pull reply-chain context before quoting.
expectations: The skill resolved the chat by name and picked the chat_id with the highest message count
The skill ran stats or participants to size up the chat before summarizing
Every quoted message includes a timestamp and sender
The skill pulled reply-chain context (via anchor-sweep or window) rather than citing bare keyword hits
The summary reports the actual date range the data covers, not the range the user asked about

evals.prompt: Find the first time <CONTACT_NAME> mentioned <TOPIC> in any conversation with me, and pull the 30 minutes of messages around that anchor so I can see the context.
evals.expected_output: A single anchor message with its reply chain. The skill should run a global keyword search (no --chat-id), pick the earliest match, then window around it.
expectations: The skill searched across all chats, not just one
The skill identified the earliest matching message and cited its timestamp
The skill pulled a window of surrounding messages around the anchor
Every quoted message includes a timestamp and sender

evals.prompt: Who are the people I have the most active Messages conversations with over the last 90 days? Unify any contacts that show up under split phone/email handles.
evals.expected_output: A ranked list of chats by message count over the window, with split-handle chats unified via participant name resolution.
expectations: The skill used a date-windowed chats query (--since) rather than all-time counts
The skill used --participant or stats to unify split phone/email chats
The response ranks results by message count
